claims.text: Confirmed missile and projectile strikes on merchant vessels in the Gulf of Oman and Strait of Hormuz approaches have elevated the JMIC maritime threat assessment to CRITICAL, effectively halting commercial transit through the corridor.

Large EU and UK offshore wind projects are facing confirmed delivery delays as Iran-linked disruption to the Hormuz shipping corridor impedes transit of turbine and blade components bound for European installation sites.

The EU Commission 2026 Energy Security Package and accelerating NIS2/CER Directive transposition has formally designated offshore wind as critical infrastructure, raising the political and strategic target profile of European wind operators.

Simultaneous constraint of both the Strait of Hormuz and Bab al-Mandab eliminates workable maritime bypass routes for Gulf-origin wind components bound for European markets, creating a structurally durable chokepoint.

Volt Typhoon, assessed by CISA and Five Eyes as a Chinese state-sponsored actor, has been confirmed pre-positioning within European critical infrastructure OT networks — consistent with long-dwell IP exfiltration tradecraft applicable to wind SCADA environments.

Sandworm, linked to Russian GRU, conducted a confirmed attack on 22 Danish energy companies in 2023 through exploitation of industrial control systems, producing operational disruption directly analogous to the offshore wind OT exposure profile.

NIS2 and CER Directive compliance gaps — with 26 EU member states subject to infringement decisions for non-transposition — create systemic OT security blind spots across wind operators that adversarial actors are assessed to be actively exploiting as an attack surface.

No confirmed Iran-attributed OT intrusion campaign specifically targeting EU wind energy assets has been identified in the current collection window, representing a material corroboration gap for a direct Iranian cyber vector against this sector.

The convergence of confirmed Hormuz disruption with nation-state OT pre-positioning in peer European energy operators creates an assessed dual-pathway risk: physical supply chain delay compounded by a plausible system intrusion vector against Southern European manufacturing and service operations.

The absence of wind-sector-specific APT reconnaissance or credential harvesting indicators in the current collection window may reflect collection gaps rather than confirmed absence of targeting activity, given confirmed pre-positioning posture in analogous European energy environments.
